title: tephroseris helenitis subsp. macrochaeta
intro: Tephroseris helenitis subsp. macrochaeta, also known as the alpine marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Tephroseris helenitis subsp. macrochaeta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, and its leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal infusions, salves, and creams for topical application.
